label: Confusion over Great Lakes' 100-mile Border Zone

comments.text: Because CBP defines each of the great lakes as an international waterway, thus its 100 mile enforcement zone begins at the lakeshore and extends 100 miles.
the fact that Chicago is considered within the border zone is ridiculous. Lake Michigan is famously the only great lake that's entirely within the US

CBP considers Chicago to be in the 100 mile zone b/c they consider all the Great Lakes' shorelines, not the actual borders, to be international borders including, absurdly, Lake Michigan. Any truly textualist or originalist jurist would see this as preposterous. www.aclu.org/documents/co...

tRump is using the Great Lakes shoreline as the starting point for the 100 mile rule. Unjustified! Also, 100 mile radius around every international airport 🛫 within the country is considered fair game for I.C.E. and border control. Unjustified!
If there's not a LAW set in stone, tRump exploits it.
how the fuck is chicago within the 200 mile border zone if lake michigan isn't considered an international lake

The blue zone is what CBP wants everyone to believe is the 100 mile border zone. The red lines delineate the actual zone based on the US External Boundary. Big differences for Great Lakes areas and DC.
I want somebody to explain to me why Lake Michigan, an entirely American pond, counts for determining the 100 mile zone
They’re asserting they’re within the 100 mile zone because of Lake Michigan. That’s obviously a profoundly dumb & dishonest claim, so why did they say it? So they could do stop & seizures in IL?

Duckworth explains why these thugs are allowed to roam so widely: they're allowed to be active within 100 air miles of a border; Lake Michigan is considered a "border." (wut) Her suggestion to limit their activities to 1 air mile of the "border" is good, though won't help much of Chicagoland.

Lake Michigan, despite being entirely within the US, is treated by ICE as international waters (even tho it absolutely isn't; only the other 4 are), and so they claim the inner shores of each of the Great Lakes, including Lake Michigan, as the starting point for the 100-mile zone measurement inland.
They’ve declared the coast of Great Lakes the border, MPLS is 150 miles from Superior. It’s 50 miles more than their 100 mile border range, but do they really care about laws?

Also, Chicago is within the 100-mile zone, because all of Lake Michigan is considered an international waterway. If this doesn't make sense, perhaps let that realization extend to all national borders. They are arbitrary lines drawn with blood and fire.
the 100 mile limit includes Lake Michigan for some stupid reason

CBP can and does operate throughout the country, and not just at airports in the interior. the 100 mile thing is a reference to increased powers close to borders (which include those with Canada and through parts of the Great Lakes), and enforcement isn’t limited to people who crossed *that* border.
CBP defines each of the Great Lakes as an international waterway, thus asserting the right to measure its 100-mile jurisdiction beginning at each lake shore, including all of Lake Michigan.
Lake Michigan is considered the border for some reason.

At the very least, this is yet more evidence in favor of my claim that the 100-mile border zone doesn't extend from the southern shore of the Great Lakes. It doesn't even if Canadians had free navigation, but if they're enforcing US law at the middle of the lake, then Chicago isn't within 100 miles.

Even according to their tortured maps, that count our edge of the Great Lakes as the “border”, Minneapolis is not within their “100-mile zone” jurisdiction - unlike dozens of other cities they could have chosen to assault. 😒

The problem is the Patriot Act decided the “border” is anywhere that is within a 100 mile radius of the border or a body of water such as the Great Lakes that border another nation… Overturn the Patriot Act as well.

This is all awful. However... What's bonkers to me is that parts of Illinois are considered part of that 100-mile zone considering that Lake Michigan is the only lake that is fully part of the US. We're not within 100 miles from any border.

Fun Fact: Because of Lake Michigan is considered to be an international waterway all of Chicago falls within the “100 Mile” exemption area which allows for searches and seizures that would otherwise be unlawful under 4th Amendment.
That is from the mistaken idea that Lake Michigan is part of the border. It is the only Great Lake which is NOT.

Also, .bsky.social, any idea if the 100-mile zone has been tested in court as applied to Chicago (which actually isn’t within 100 miles of an external border since Lake Michigan is entirely within the US)?
The entire state of Michigan is a warrantless border zone according to CBP because every inch of it falls within a 100 mile radius of the border (all of the Great Lakes are considered a border)
You're correct, but that's not how the 100-mile zone is defined. It includes the shores of the Great Lakes. Don't think immigration borders. Think "catching alcohol smugglers during Prohibition".

It's also contiguous with the other Great Lakes, which is probably why the shore is treated as a border for immigration purposes. When CPB went inyo Charlotte, there was no such excuse. This is just info. "Why" =/= "should."
The Great Lakes are considered a border zone. www.michiganpublic.org/news/2018-04...
"border" includes great lakes + all the coast + canada border + 100 mile radius lol So they're staying in MN lol

I know that is also likely a thing, but I am specifically referencing the fact that the 100 Mile Zone maps include all of area around western and southern Lake Michigan that are actually more than 100 miles away from the border. The map doesn't show buffer zones around international airports

I thought Chicago was on the 100 mile zone because of Lake Michigan. I know it’s not an international border but I thought that zone was in effect because the lake is such a huge part of the US border. (Why my state of Michigan is basically fully in the zone.)

This will never not be completely insane. Most Americans live in the border zone. I'll also note that Chicago is only in this zone if you consider Lake Michigan to be the border, but it's an entirely American pond.

That's been the "border zone" since the 1950s, bizarrely counting the shore of Lake Michigan (which is 100% inside the US) as part of the border, so that places like Chicago, 400 miles from the border, and all of Michigan are included.
And the Great Lakes, so the middle of the country. People forget that as well. Chicago falls within the 100-mile zone that is a maritime border on the Great Lakes. So the border laws apply there too.

The reg says external boundary = land boundary or "territorial sea" and while the Great Lakes have many different kinds of fed jur statuses, I'm not aware of them ever being a "territorial sea" - other terms are used. www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/8/2...

As far as I can tell, for CBP purposes Congress uses the "external boundary" of the country and does not include the Great Lakes. Minneapolis and Chicago aren't in the 100-air mile band. www.congress.gov/crs-product/...
see below: The 100-mile U.S. "Border" also encircles the Great Lakes.

The border isn't just the US/Mexico border. LA is on the Pacific coast, and the entire coastline of the US is considered a border. Even Chicago, on lake Michigan, is technically within 100 miles of the border since the 100 mile limitation doesn't begin until dry land.

Oh yes, and the "border" extends 100 km inland and includes internal waters like Lake Michigan so that 80% of Americans live "on the border" and can be treated like they're going through airport security at all times.
Chicago is not in the “100-mile border zone”, you brain donors. Lake Michigan lies entirely within the borders of the USA, and Chicago is famously a 333-mile boat ride south from Mackinac Island.
The U.S. border runs along the edges of Lake Michigan so Chicago is within that 100 mile zone.

Airports? Lake Michigan? Not borders; don't have to be for CBP to function. Next up we'll see people claiming that the government thinks the border zone extends down from space and therefore the entire country's in it.
How about also following the law and the Constitution? No CBP beyond 100 from the border, and Lake Michigan is entirely within the US. Need I go on?

Invariably, this map is shown to include the Great Lakes as if they were the high seas, therefore putting places large sections of the Midwest within the 100-mile band. I have no reason to believe the ACLU is wrong over what CBP considers the border to be. But this is ridiculous. 2/

Another thing to consider: There’s 100-mile zone extending from every border edge of the US(Including the inner shoreline of the Great Lakes) where Customs and Border Protection can act in basically a completely unconstitutional way as long as they can pretext it as being an enforcement action. 1/

A 1953 federal regulation interpreting the words "reasonable distance" in a 1946 Immigration and Nationality Act created a 100 mile Constitution-Free Zone for the Border Patrol. Here's what the 100 mile Constitution-Free Zone looks like in the Great Lakes region
Isn't this 100-mile zone "rule" used to justify Border Control in Illinois an admission by the US government that the Great Lakes belong to Canada?: www.milwaukeeindependent.com/featured/bey...
ICE technically has a 100 mile radius of enforcement on all US borders. The bastards have access to all the Great Lakes cities as a result.
Has anyone ever challenged their definition of the 100-mile buffer zone? It *should* be from the territorial limit, not the edge of, say, the Chesapeake Bay or Lake Michigan.
I have never understood how Lake Michigan qualifies as an international border so that Chicago is in the 100 mile zone. It's the only Great Lake entirely within US borders.
The 100-mile zone is such bullshit. Especially since it includes me and I’m not 100 miles within a border at all. But the shores of Lake Michigan count for some reason
Lake Michigan is entirely US territorial waters. Why is Milwaukee even in the ICE jurisdiction? It is no where near the bullshit 100 mile boundary of ICE jurisdiction?

Context: US Customs & Border Patrol (CBP) has a 100 mile border zone where it has expanded jurisdiction to warrantless stop people. I just don’t know who has an official map & official rules on where a CBP zone border actually is. Lake Michigan in US internal waters!
www.aclu.org/know-your-ri...

I am still baffled that Chicago got written into the 100 mile zone when it is over 200 miles from the actual border. It's like they ignored Lake Michigan being fully inside US borders simply so they could include that area...

It still pisses me off that they included the shores of lake Michigan as 'the border' when lake Michigan is the only great lake ENTIRELY WITHIN THE BOUNDARIES OF THE US., They did that to capture chicago in their terror zone, of course 😡

Who knew the shores of Lake Michigan were considered the border by Customs & Border Protection. Should small boat owners be concerned? btw, another beautiful Fall day in Illinois. youtu.be/vDIRfRqlnIQ?...
The entire coastline is regarded as the border, even including the coastline of Lake Michigan, which is wholly inside the US.

ICE and CBP can act anywhere in the country. The 100 mile zone only grants CBP authority to search and arrest based on reasonable suspicion instead of probable cause. Lake Michigan is not an international border, by the way. That's hundreds of miles north in the middle of Lake Superior.
Also, Lake Michigan is entirely within the US; it is not international waters, so the 100 mile zone in eastern WI, IL, IN and east shore of MI should not be highlighted.
If Lake Michigan is considered ‘border’ than so should the seaport in Lewiston ID because the Columbia River is also navigable in the same definitions.

DHS considers Lake Michigan shore a border zone & they sure don't seem to give a damn about 4A or what Congress said in 2021. I've personally seen CBP vehicles on the far west side of Michigan in an area not highlighted on the Congress map. Even if we go by it that's a fuckton of the US population.

I've been wondering about that 100-mile envelope. I know CBP claimed that the shore of Lake Michigan was a border and therefore they could operate in Chicago (obvious BS), but even by the most generous possible reading of that standard the downtowns of MSP are outside that.
Someone tell us why Lake Michigan is a border zone

ALL OF MICHIGAN falls under a federal statute that allows warrantless searches within 100 air miles from any external boundary, including coastal boundaries and considers the entire state of Michigan as falling within the 100-mile zone. If your state borders the Great Lakes, gird up.

All of the Great Lakes are considered a US border zone. This is nothing new - the law has been in effect since 1953, and the map was originally drawn during Prohibition. SCOTUS upheld Border Patrol checkpoints wholly within the US in US vs Martinez-Fuente.

Just a thought: the difference between the Great Lakes area and rest of the US coast is that the border zone doesn’t include most of Lake Michigan, whereas Chesapeake Bay and other areas are already included. Why declare a functional border at a location that already is in the border zone?
Even if the Great Lakes were taken to be the high seas, by regulation, the border zone would still include 12 nautical miles of territorial sea from the baseline at the shore of the lakes. 4/

Our agreements with Canada and her predecessors provide that the Great Lakes are internal waters. The boundary runs up the middle of most of them (and not through Lake Michigan at all). The 100-mile zone measures from _there_. 3/

Every international airport and any coastal area, even the great lakes and Chicago are in that 100 mile zone. So there is almost no area in the US that won't be argued by DOJ/DHS to fall under that jurisdiction.

The entire state of Michigan is considered a border zone by US Customs and Border Protection because the state is surrounded by international borders or waterways, including the Great Lakes and Canada. CBP can enforce immigration laws anywhere within the state.

Incursion of 100 miles was designated in Trump 1.0. Border zone was designated during NAFTA, and had a zone of 52 miles (26 on either side of the border). Coasts were also expanded. Great Lakes (WI, IL, MI, IN) coast Lake Michigan is not Int’l, all US territory. This applies in other regions.

Don't give them ideas. We don't need these bozos in the Detroit River. But note that it isn't a Trump thing that Chicago is officially in the "border zone". Anything within 100 miles (maybe more--they might have changed it) of the Great Lakes is considered to be on the border.
The government unfortunately considers the Great Lakes international waterways, and gives CBP a 100 mile inland jurisdiction.

SCOTUS gave CBP and DHS the authority to operate within a 100 mile zone of the US border. I want to clarify what the 100 mile zone is. You have international borders with the Great Lakes, O’Hare airport CBP, and the border with Canada.

It's important to note that CBP has a 100 mile jurisdiction along all of our international borders. This means that agents are authorized to detain individuals up to 100 miles inland. The Great Lakes are considered a boundary, so Chicago is included. Most Americans live in this zone.

The Great Lakes are considered an international waterway, so here in Michigan, for example, just about the entire state gets considered within the 100 mile limit. Many parts of the country you would never consider qualify

See.. they like to justify their activity band by coast line measurements EVEN on the Great Lakes firmly inside US territory lines whereas their 100 mile border stuff should be off the US/Canada border.

Being a Michigan resident: I’ve been told that the entirety of the state is considered to be within that 100 mile radius, due to the Great Lakes being considered international waterways (?) or something like that. MSP area would fall into that as well probably, same reasoning.

CBP is allowed to make border enforcement arrests within 100 air miles of a territorial border. The Great Lakes are considered part of this zone--including Chicago and Milwaukee. So CBP actions along the **Northern border** are likely part of his remit. www.arcgis.com/apps/mapview...

Federal regulations apparently count coastlines in that 100-mile area. So e.g. Chicago is included bc of its proximity to the Great Lakes rather than to Canada. There's def room to legally pare back this allowance.
And while we’re at it, Michigan is a “border state” like Arizona is a “Great Lakes” state.
As I've said before, the boundary is along US 8 in the west, then dropping to WI 64, then again almost but not quite to US 10 in the east, reaching Lake Michigan just north of Green Bay

REGULAR REMINDER (well first for Bluesky but been yelling for years on birdsite) ALL OF MICHIGAN IS A "BORDER ZONE". In case the Canadians sail down Lake Michigan apparently. Which is f--ked up. www.aclumich.org/en/cases/all...

When your attorney in Chicago is informed that the city is considered within the 100-mile border zone because it is adjacent to Lake Michigan, which is classified as an “external boundary” of the United States: “Crazy”

As Lake Michigan & Chesapeake Bay Maryland are entirely inside the US, both Chicago IL & Washington DC should be nowhere near the 100 mile CPB zone if that 100 mile zone only starts in international water or foreign countries. I assume CBP zone is way more complicated, but ACLU map feels made-up.

I could have sworn I read that somewhere (but of course can't find it now). Including lake Michigan as a "maritime" border does seem arbitrary, but how else could they shoehorn another major metropolitan area into the "We do what we want" zone?
Correction: it's considered part of the 100-mile border zone. You're right that by dint of Michigan we're not quite there. But lake Michigan is still considered a border I guess.

Broadview is roughly 13 miles from Chicago, on Lake Michigan. That puts it smack in the middle of the 100 mile boundary limit - which CBP controls. ICE does not. That's a problem for ICE. www.internationalboundarycommission.org/en/maps-coor...

.bsky.social .bsky.social .bsky.social One of you talked about the border zone recently, and how it's often misunderstood. If Lake Michigan is in fact *not* an international water, then Chicago is not in the zone. ACLU says it is. www.aclu.org/know-your-ri...

CBP can operate within 100 miles of an international boundary or port of entry. That "boundary" includes the shoreline of Lake Michigan. But even without that, outside of the "100-mile zone", CBP can act as immigration enforcement. They just don't have the unfettered rights they do in the zone.
"The boundary with Canada also does not run through Lake Michigan, so the 100-mile zone does not include any place in Illinois."
Actually Lake Michigan is a Great Lake. And using the Great Lake network, it's considered a border. Has been for years.

I believe the 100 mile border rule was some arbitrary number that the DOJ came up with in 1953. Rule made without public comment or debate and without input from Congress. And the bottom of Lake Michigan as a border is ridiculous - Chicago is over 500 miles from the Canadian border.

What's the excuse for CPB's warrantless searches in Charlotte, given it's way outside the 100-mile border zone? Here in Chicago they tried to argue that Lake Michigan is an international border. I'm curious if they have a similar BS argument, or have just given up on even the pretense of legality.

Red zone is not at scale. Charlotte is 170 mi from the coastal border & while Chicago is on the shore of Lake Michigan, the lake shares no border w/Canada. In 1953, the DOJ granted the CBP authority to conduct warrantless searches up to 100 mi of the border, only w/cause, for immigration violations.
the 100 mile zone is bullshit on principle, but saying that the shores of Lake Michigan count as an external boundary is extra fucked imo
Chicago is a city within the 100 mile border zone somehow, because it’s on Lake Michigan, even though that lake is entirely within the US. Don’t understand, but it is in CBP 100 mile enforcement zone
Chicago is most definitely in their zone since the shore of Lake Michigan counts as a boundary.

For the purposes of CBP, the "border" starts at the coastline, including the coasts of lake michigan. Several entire states are exempt from the 4th amendment if it's CBP doing the search. That said, I don't think Minneapolis is within the zone. And regardless, the feds no longer care about laws so..

It's not 100 miles from an airport.... the fact people believe this and lets it happen is the real problem. Lord knows I've had enough arguments around here and said that... and ACLU's map showing Chicago and parts of WI near Lake Michigan as that 100 mile boundary is wrong also.
I believe Lake Michigan is considered a border in this case. 😡

The Canadian border runs down the middle of Lake Huron and then Lake Erie. So yes, all of Michigan is in a border zone. To answer your other question. It's "for" raising awareness. ICE can detain you for no reason in that zone, especially if you're a brown person. So maybe warn any POC friends...

I.C.E. also ignores this restriction on their "enforcement zone".(among the many MANY laws they violate) Notice that Minneapolis is not in that 100 mile area(It's 130 miles from Lake Superior). I live 145 miles from the border and I.C.E. has been operating in my area. They're probably in Kansas.
